lesson.big_idea: Force and pressure help us explain how pushes and pulls act on surfaces. When the same force spreads over a smaller area, pressure rises.

sections.title: Opening
sections.body: We meet force as a push or pull.

sections.title: Understanding Force
sections.body: A force is a push or a pull. It can start, stop, or change motion.

sections.title: Worked Example
sections.body: Compare a sharp pin and a blunt eraser under the same push.

sections.title: Summary
sections.body: Force acts; pressure depends on area.

sections.title: Reflect
sections.body: Where do you notice pressure at home?

lesson.svg_diagram: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="200" height="80" viewBox="0 0 200 80"><rect x="10" y="20" width="180" height="40" rx="12" fill="#e6f7f8"/><text x="100" y="45" text-anchor="middle">Force → Area → Pressure</text></svg>
